binary/ternary FY
Angular anisotropy of fission fragments in ternary
fission of U-235 induced by 3 MeV neutrons
5.5 MeV Van de Graaff for 3 MeV neutrons
Apsara reactor for some thermal neutron
measurement
Neutron energy spread about 200 keV, calculated
from tritium target thickness.
Metallic U-235 of about 1.5 cm2 area and 1 mg/cm2
thickness was coated on a 10 millig/cm2 Al backing
Two independent methods. In the first method a gridded
ionisation chamber was used to measure the direction of
emission of fission fragments and a CsI(Tl) crystal for
detecting long range alphas. In the second one semicon-
ductor detectors were used for detecting both fission
fragments and long-range alphas,using coincidence and
anticoincidence set-up
CsI(Tl) crystal for detecting LRA
gridded ionization chamber for fragments
semi-conductor detectors for LRA and fragms.
Angular anisotropies expressed by ratio of two measured
intensities n(ang). Ratio at 3 MeV obtained on the
basis of isotropy for the case of thermal neutron
binary fission
calibration of collector pulse
heights into fragment energies done using known
energies of the light and heavy fragments peaks.
No information
ML. Revision of references, all subentries
converted to reaction formalism. The following
subentries were deleted for reasons given below
subent 8 = contained a quantity and value in the data
table which was not measured but taken from liter-
ature. The original contents of subent 8 were re-
placed by data originally included in subent 6.
subent 9 = contained no data table, but in a comment
some qualitative description of the energy spectrum
of alpha particles in 3 MeV neutron fission, taken
from a progress report (AEET-228,page 4).
Furthermore,in the final publication no information
on any measurement of alpha-spectra is given.
subent 10 = contained a deduced value related to the
data in subent 11. It is now included in a comment
of subent 11 (refer to it for details).
ML. Minor corrections
BIB section updated
SD: REACTION code in Subent 007 was changed
according to EXFOR rules.
VS. Reference 65SALZBURG,2,397,1965 added.
010 restored and updated. 011 REACTION corrected.
Angular anisotropy ratio n(7-deg)/n(80deg) for binary
fission measured with ionisation chamber and CsI set-up
assuming isotropy for thermal neutron binary fission.
Note = in the text of the publication (see 'status')
the angles are given as 7 and 85 deg, in table 1
as 17 and 80 deg respectively.

Angular anisotropy ratio n(0-deg)/n(90deg) for binary
fission measured with semiconductor detectors
assuming isotropy for thermal neutron binary fission.

OS.
ML.Converted to reaction,90-deg data deleted
Dataset was restored.
Observed anisotropy for the first set of measurements
with a distance source-sample of 20 cm was 1.11+-0.01.
Observed anisotropy for the second set of measurements
with a distance source-sample of 7 cm was 1.04+-0.01.
Observed anisotropy for thermal neutrons was, due to
preferential detection at 90.deg, 0.99+-0.01.
Final result was corrected for nonuniform neutron flux
and finite angular resolution.

preferential detection at 90.deg, 0.75+-0.08.
